name: holospaces egress
description: Local egress for holospaces guests. A browser tab has no raw sockets; this extension opens them for a holospace's network via the Direct Sockets API, so a Chromebook's devcontainer reaches the internet with no node, no relay, and no proxy. It speaks the same egress protocol (CC-16) the browser already uses for a holospaces-node (CC-39) — a local egress node in the browser.
_comment_permissions: Direct Sockets (TCPSocket/UDPSocket) is a powerful, gated capability — it lets the service worker open raw sockets to arbitrary hosts. Chrome exposes it to extensions that declare it; depending on the channel it may require an enterprise policy or chrome://flags (Direct Sockets). host_permissions additionally grants CORS-free fetch() for the content path (pulling CORS-blocked registries/CDNs as κ-content).
_comment_connect: Only the operator's own holospaces origins may talk to this extension — the egress is the operator's, self-sovereign, not open to any page.
